data.title: Birks Group receives non-compliance notice from NYSE American
data.description:
• Birks Group (NYSE:BGI) received notice from the NYSE American on February 25, 2025 that it was not in compliance with the continued listing rules, the company said on Friday.
  • This happens if a listed company has stockholders’ equity of less than U.S. $2.0M and has reported losses from continuing operations and/or net losses in two of its three most recent fiscal years and if a listed company has stockholders’ equity of less than U.S. $4.0M and has reported losses from continuing operations and/or net losses in three of its four most recent fiscal years.
  • The company reported stockholders’ deficit of about $(6.1)M as of September 28, 2024, and has reported net losses in two of its three most recent fiscal years ended March 30, 2024 and three of its four most recent fiscal years ended March 30, 2024.
  • Birks must submit a plan to NYSE American by March 27, 2025 addressing how it intends to regain compliance, failing to which the company will be subject to delisting proceedings.
  • If the plan is accepted, the company will also be subject to periodic NYSE American reviews, including quarterly monitoring for compliance with the plan.
  • BGI +5.93% after hours to $1.25.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Green Plains CEO to depart
data.description:
• Green Plains (NASDAQ:GPRE) said on Friday that its President and Chief Executive Officer, Todd Becker, is departing after more than 17 years at the company, effective March 1, 2025.
  • Pursuant to its CEO succession plan, the board has engaged a leading executive search firm to help identify a new CEO.
  • The Board has also created an Executive Committee, comprised of Imre Havasi, Senior Vice President – Head of Trading and Commercial Operations, Michelle Mapes, Chief Legal & Administration Officer, Chris Osowski, Executive Vice President – Operations and Technology, and Jamie Herbert, Chief Human Resources Officer, to lead the company until Mr. Becker’s successor is appointed.
  • The company also announced that Patrich Simpkins will move from Chief Transformation Officer of Green Plains to Chief Executive Officer of Fluid Quip Technologies, a majority owned subsidiary of the company.
  • Green Plains (GPRE) shares were up 1.70% in post-market trading.

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare shareholders OK acquisition by Aya
data.description:
• Cross Country Healthcare (NASDAQ:CCRN) shareholders have approved the company's planned acquisition by Aya Healthcare.
  • The deal is expected to close in the second half of the year.
  • The companies hit a bump earlier in February after a second request from the Federal Trade Commission for more information regarding the deal.

data.title: Toro to spin off its Handysize tanker business; shares climb
data.description:
Toro (NASDAQ:TORO) said on Friday that it has decided, on the recommendation of the board, to effect a spin-off of its Handysize tanker business comprising one Handysize tanker and Xavier Shipping.

In the spin-off, Toro shareholders will receive one common share of Robin Energy, a newly formed subsidiary that will act as the holding company for the one tanker vessel, for every eight Toro common shares.

Robin has applied to have its common shares listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

Toro’s Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Petros Panagiotidis, has been appointed as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Robin with effect as of the completion of the spin-off.

The board believes that the creation of a business in a distinct sector of the shipping industry – Handysize Product Tankers– will provide significant benefits to both companies and their shareholders.

The transaction is expected to enable each of Toro and Robin to increase its focus on their respective line of businesses, enhance operational efficiencies, facilitate efficient strategic expansion, attract new investors, and, with this dividend distribution of Robin common shares, give Toro shareholders the flexibility to monetize or adjust their equity holdings.

Toro (TORO) shares were up more than 8% in extended trading on Friday.

data.title: S&P 500 February winners, losers as stock benchmark ends monthly journey in the red
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) hit new highs during February trade, but that wasn’t enough to stave off a monthly loss.

The benchmark (SP500)(NYSEARCA:VOO)(NYSEARCA:IVV) ended the month’s final session in the win column, but concluded February down by -1.4%. Among key factors swaying stocks this month was the Trump administration’s flurry of tariff actions against trading partners Mexico, China and Canada.

In the index (SP500)(NYSEARCA:SPY), Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) led gainers in an eventful month for the AI server company. Its stock faced a Nasdaq delisting, but the company this week filed its delayed financial reports to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) paced decliners, heading into February trade with a slight miss in its FY 25 revenue outlook, while Q3 results beat Wall Street’s estimates. The footwear maker behind UGG, Teva, and Hoka, is part of the S&P 500 Consumer Discretionary sector (NYSEARCA:XLY). The sector was the worst-performing on the benchmark (SP500) in February, down -10%.

Here are the S&P 500's (SP500) biggest percentage gainers and decliners for February, with data from Capital IQ.

Top 5 percentage gainers:

  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI): +48.8%
  • Yum Brands (NYSE:YUM): +21.1%
  • Intel (NASDAQ:INTC): +20.2%
  • Philip Morris (NYSE:PM): +19.8%
  • Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD): +19.6%

Top 5 percentage decliners:

  • Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK): -36.3%
  • FMC Corporation (NYSE:FMC): -34.5%
  • West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST): -31.6%
  • Celanese (NYSE:CE): -28.2%
  • Teleflex (NYSE:TFX): -26.5%

data.title: Denali Therapeutics closes down 11% as 2025 operating expenses seen weighing
data.description:
• Denali Therapeutics (NASDAQ:DNLI) closed down 11% Friday as investors seemed concerned about the biopharma's projected rise in operating expenses for 2025.
  • In its Q4 and full-year 2024 financial results released after Thursday's closing bell, the company said it anticipated a 10%-15% increase in cash operating expenses in 2025 compared to last year.
  • Denali did beat on EPS in Q4. It did not report any revenue for the quarter or 2024.
  • Although he maintained his buy rating, HC Wainwright analyst Andrew Fein lowered his price target to $80 from $87 (~383% upside based on Feb. 28 close).
  • Denali has previously said it expects to submit a BLA for its lead candidate, videnofusp alfa (DNL310), for Hunter syndrome, in early 2025 with potential approval by the end of the year.
